disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lesions began about 48 hours ago as a single small red sore under the nose following a mild upper respiratory infection, then spread rapidly over the last 12 hours
trigger_question_examples: When did the sores start and how have they changed?
Did anything come before them, like a cold?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started about two days ago as one little red spot under the nose, just after they had a mild cold, and in the last 12 hours it's spread really fast.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Thick golden, honey-coloured crusts weeping yellow fluid on an erythematous base around the nose and mouth, with satellite lesions on the chin from autoinoculation via scratching
trigger_question_examples: What do the sores look like — the colour, any fluid or crust?
Have new spots appeared anywhere else?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're covered in thick golden, honey-coloured crusts on red skin and keep weeping a clear yellow fluid, and a couple of new matching spots have popped up on the chin where they've been scratching.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The sores are localised to the perioral/perinasal area and chin and are not showing a spreading, firm, advancing red edge across the cheeks toward the eyes; no deep hard swelling
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness spreading out across the cheeks or toward the eyes with a firm edge?
Is the skin around it hot, hard or deeply swollen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The sores stay around the nose, mouth and chin — the redness isn't racing across the cheeks or up toward the eyes, and the skin around it isn't hard or deeply swollen, it's just the crusty patch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No high fever, no rigors, no lethargy; the child feels only slightly warm, is still up and about and behaving fairly normally, though eating less due to local pain
trigger_question_examples: Have they had any fevers, shakes or shivers?
Are they floppy or unusually sleepy, or still themselves?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No proper fever or shivers — they feel a bit warm but that's all, and apart from being sore and eating less they're still up and about and their usual self.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Grouped clear vesicles, a tingling/neuropathic prodrome, and punched-out monomorphic erosions are absent — arguing against a herpes simplex picture in favour of a crusted bacterial one
trigger_question_examples: Before the crusts, were there little clear blisters grouped together, or any tingling?
Do the sores look like lots of identical little punched-out ulcers?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there weren't little clear blisters or any tingling beforehand, and they don't look like lots of identical little ulcers — it's more of a spreading crusty golden patch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High household transmission risk — siblings at home and shared hand towels; child excluded from childcare because of contagion
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone else at home, and are you sharing towels or face cloths?
Is the child still going to childcare or school?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There are siblings at home and we've been sharing hand towels, and I've kept them home from childcare because I know it's really catching.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Local pain from tightening crusts is reducing oral intake, but the child is still drinking and passing urine normally (no dehydration)
trigger_question_examples: Is the soreness stopping them eating or drinking?
Are they still drinking and weeing normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The sore crusts make eating hurt so they're picking at food, but they're still drinking okay and weeing normally.
